Mission Control for permit intelligence.

Turn fragmented public records into evidence-backed permit review packets.

Mission Control / Arroyo Vista ADU Resubmittal Ready for review

One case record connects every source, event, finding, review decision, and packet revision.

  • 9 evidence records
  • 8 timeline events

✓Evidence-backedClaims stay connected to their sources.

✓Reviewer approvedHuman judgment controls client findings.

✓Deterministic outputOne reviewed model drives every packet.

What happens after I submit?

A clear path from scattered records to a professional deliverable.

Within one workflow, the case moves from intake through evidence review and human judgment to a source-linked packet.

  1. 01
    ⌁

    Mission Control

    Your project becomes a structured case with visible investigation health, open questions, and packet readiness.

  2. 02
    ▤

    Evidence Review

    Records are organized, dated, source-labeled, and reviewed so facts stay separate from assumptions.

  3. 03
    ⌖

    Timeline + Findings

    Material events are reconstructed and reviewer-approved findings remain linked to supporting evidence.

  4. 04
    ▧

    Professional Review Packet

    The reviewed case renders into a consistent client deliverable—without implying agency approval.

    Open the 14-page sample ↗

Evidence Review / source provenance

Every conclusion starts with a record.

Portal captures, correction notices, receipts, correspondence, and analyst notes enter one evidence register. Each record retains its source, date, contributor, provenance, and verification state.

  • Verified, awaiting-review, and disputed states remain visible
  • Source facts stay separate from assumptions and analysis
  • Timeline events and findings cite their supporting records

Timeline / traceability

The chronology stays connected on screen and on paper.

Mission Control links every material event to evidence. The same reviewed chronology appears in the final packet with source linkage and review status preserved.

Findings / human review

Software organizes the record. People approve the judgment.

PermitPulse can assist with classification, chronology, and drafting, but client-facing findings and recommended actions remain a human review decision.

01Evidence is reviewed

02Draft findings are grounded

03A reviewer approves delivery

No approval promises. Packet Readiness means the deliverable is complete; it does not mean a jurisdiction has resolved or approved the permit.

The professional deliverable

One packet. Every important layer of the review.

The canonical sample is a 14-page fictional demonstration showing how reviewed records become a clear, source-linked client artifact.

Download Sample Packet ↗ Request a packet walkthrough by email →

Trust by design

Traceable inputs. Controlled judgment. Reproducible output.

Every layer is designed to make the basis, reviewer state, and limits of the work visible—not to manufacture certainty that the record does not support.

01

Evidence-backed

Material statements point back to reviewed source records.

02

Source provenance

Source label, date, contributor, and digital provenance travel with the evidence.

03

Reviewer-approved findings

Client findings and actions require recorded human review.

04

Deterministic rendering

Preview, HTML, text, and PDF consume one canonical presentation model.

05

Cloudflare architecture

Authenticated Workers coordinate D1 case records and private R2 evidence storage.

06

Better Auth access

Workspace sessions and roles gate access to client and reviewer operations.

Important: PermitPulse supports research, organization, review, and delivery. It does not guarantee permit approval, replace jurisdiction confirmation, or substitute for legal, architectural, or engineering advice.
